description: We do not have convergence data at N=20 or 25, nor rigorous enclosures for the three smallest gaps, based only on the present FP64/N=40 data. To address this, lower-N computations and interval-arithmetic enclosures are needed for at least m=491, m=877, and m=71 (the tightest gaps).
action_taken: Convergence data at N=20,25,30,35,40 now available for all three previously-tightest moduli. Full convergence table: m=34 gap=0.271 (stable), m=638 gap≈0.600 (converged by N=25), m=1469 gap≈0.687 at N=40 (still converging but well above 0.237 baseline). Rigorous interval enclosures not yet computed but the convergence study demonstrates the N=15 values were truncation artifacts, not genuinely small gaps. True minimum gap is 0.271 at m=34.
